text.title: Coverage Workflow and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Coverage-driven test loop, diff-cover for PRs, CI integration, and agent failure modes with mitigations.

rule.statement: Feed machine-readable coverage reports (coverage.xml, lcov.info) to the test-author subagent; do not paste the full XML — extract only uncovered lines per touched file.
rule.rationale: Full XML reports are hundreds of kilobytes; extracting uncovered lines reduces token cost and prevents context saturation.

list.item: Step 1: run suite with branch coverage and machine-readable output.
Step 2: extract uncovered lines per touched file; feed to test-author subagent with the source file pinned in context.
Step 3: agent writes targeted tests, asserting behavior (not just calling the function).
Step 4: re-run coverage; if delta is less than expected, repeat or escalate to human.

section.title: Diff Coverage for PRs

rule.statement: Enforce diff-coverage on changed lines (target 80–95%) rather than repo-wide percentage; legacy gaps must not block ongoing work.
rule.rationale: Repo-wide gates punish new work for historical debt; diff-coverage focuses the requirement on the changes being reviewed.

section.reference: Run pytest + diff-cover and emit per-file uncovered lines as agent-ready prompt fragments.

rule.statement: Combine coverage with mutation testing on critical modules (auth, billing, data integrity) quarterly; coverage measures execution, mutation measures assertion strength.
rule.rationale: A test that calls a function but asserts nothing shows 100% line coverage; mutation testing detects missing assertions.

rule.statement: Persist HTML coverage reports as CI artifacts; do not require a SaaS coverage service for the core gate.
rule.rationale: SaaS dependency adds failure modes; artifacts are simpler and always available to the agent during post-mortem.

antipattern.description: Coverage gaming: agent writes tests that call functions and ignore return values.
antipattern.reason: Require at least one explicit assert/expect per test; enforce with pytest-flake8-assertions or eslint-plugin-jest/expect-expect.

antipattern.description: Threshold ratcheting: agent adds pragma: no cover to changed files to pass the gate.
antipattern.reason: Fail the build if pragmas are added to changed files unless explicitly justified in the PR body.

antipattern.description: Agent lowering a coverage threshold to make CI green.
antipattern.reason: Threshold changes require a labelled PR with human sign-off; never let an agent modify thresholds autonomously.

antipattern.description: Async coverage gaps: pytest-asyncio or Jest fake timers need extra config; agent does not notice.
antipattern.reason: Provide a complete coverage-config snippet in the test-writing prompt; do not rely on the agent inferring async requirements.
